meta.note: South Africa Stratum A batch 2. Deduped against the 37 existing + 27 pilot ZA titles. Includes titles now sourced at title-level that were held at author-level in the pilot (Tell Freedom). Black Like Me attaches to existing book id 3484. Held: To Every Birth Its Blood (McDonald's table marks it 'Passed' in one place, banned in another — source conflict), One Time Brother (play, uncited), In a Province (unverified). Excluded as contradicted/not-banned: Cronin's Inside, Mzamane's Mzala, Nakasa, Essop's The Hajji (all 'Passed' or prize-winning). Primary sources: theliteraturepolice.com (McDonald, Webnotes E/P), SAHO, PEN America.

entries.title: Tell Freedom
entries.description_ban: Abrahams's memoir of his Johannesburg childhood; banned in South Africa in 1957 — described by PEN America as the first work by a Black author to be banned in the country.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by PEN America (now sourced at title level, unlike the pilot).
entries.source_name: PEN America — Surveillance in South Africa

entries.title: Tsetlo
entries.description_ban: Serote's second Black Consciousness poetry collection; banned by the South African authorities.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; named in biographical encyclopedia entries (medium confidence).

entries.title: African Songs
entries.description_ban: Rive's first short-story collection (including 'The Bench'); banned in South Africa under the prohibition on anti-apartheid writing after his 1963 return.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by South African History Online.

entries.title: Quartet: New Voices from South Africa
entries.description_ban: Anthology of South African short fiction edited by Rive; banned by the apartheid government.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by SAHO and Sunday Times Books.

entries.title: Cross of Gold
entries.description_ban: Ngcobo's debut novel of Black life after Sharpeville; promptly banned and never circulated in South Africa while she was in exile in England.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by the Johannesburg Review of Books and Encyclopedia.com.

entries.title: He's My Brother
entries.description_ban: Novel about a teacher's affairs with a jailed activist and a man later murdered; the ban drove Roberts into US exile. Title attribution contested (the US edition was titled 'Johannesburg Requiem').
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by Encyclopedia.com (medium confidence; title attribution noted).

entries.title: Where White is the Colour, Where Black is the Number
entries.description_ban: Avant-garde Black Consciousness poetry collection (Ravan Press) by a white poet who had himself reclassified as Black; banned by the apartheid authorities for its treatment of race.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by South African History Online.

entries.title: A Seed Must Seem to Die
entries.description_ban: Ravan Press Staffrider-series poetry collection by Daniel P. Kunene (distinct from Mazisi Kunene); listed 'Banned' in McDonald's Literature Police Staffrider table.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; listed in McDonald's Literature Police Webnote P (medium confidence).

entries.title: The Best of Henry Miller
entries.description_ban: Henry Miller anthology edited by Lawrence Durrell; banned by South African censors in 1966 (Publications Control Board decision ref. BCS 790/65).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban with a decision reference number; from McDonald's Literature Police Webnote E (medium confidence).
entries.source_name: The Literature Police (McDonald), Webnote E (ref. BCS 790/65)

entries.title: 117 Days
entries.description_ban: Memoir of 117 days' solitary detention under the 90-day law; First was a banned person, so her publications were prohibited from circulation. Exact Gazette year for the title not pinned down.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific memoir prohibited under the author's banning; documented via Wikipedia and McDonald (medium confidence).

entries.title: Bandiet
entries.description_ban: Prison memoir (Lewin jailed for African Resistance Movement sabotage); published in London in 1974 and banned in South Africa for many years, republished locally only in 2002.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban of a prison memoir; documented via Wikipedia (medium confidence).

entries.title: Amandla
entries.description_ban: Tlali's Black Consciousness novel of the Soweto uprising (Ravan, Staffrider series); listed 'Banned' in McDonald's Literature Police Staffrider table. Distinct from her earlier Muriel at Metropolitan.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; listed in McDonald's Literature Police Webnote P.

entries.title: Black Like Me
entries.description_ban: American journalist's account of passing as Black; banned by South African censors in 1966 (Publications Control Board decision ref. BCS 1033/65). Adds a ZA ban to the existing book record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a South Africa ban to an existing book; documented with a decision reference in McDonald's Literature Police Webnote E.
entries.source_name: The Literature Police (McDonald), Webnote E (ref. BCS 1033/65)
